title: Blurb

jokes.text: Well, that just tickles my twat!
jokes.setup: Hearing an eloquent, inspiring blurb about herself
jokes.punchline: Responding with crude sexual language that completely undermines the dignity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina - takes something beautiful and immediately makes it about her genitals. The contrast between the lofty language and her response is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: And Mike. - See, Mike? That is good writing.
jokes.setup: Listing 200 of Washington's most important people
jokes.punchline: Adding Mike as an afterthought, then using him as a teaching moment
jokes.explanation: Clean setup/punchline that establishes Mike's low status while Selina uses it to insult his writing. Efficient character work.

jokes.text: So, today. That's a bit of a brain twister.
jokes.setup: The day after yesterday
jokes.punchline: Treating simple math as confusing
jokes.explanation: Pure Richard - overthinking the simplest concepts. Character-specific humor that lands softly but consistently.

jokes.text: Richard, you have such a good sense of humor. Amy, you should take a cue from him, really.
jokes.setup: Richard just said something unintentionally funny
jokes.punchline: Selina praising him while insulting Amy
jokes.explanation: Selina using Richard's accidental humor to take a shot at Amy. Typical dynamic but well-executed.

jokes.text: Do you have some bai hao white tip? - I do!
jokes.setup: Pretentious editor asking for obscure tea
jokes.punchline: Selina immediately claiming to have it despite clearly not knowing what it is
jokes.explanation: Selina's desperate need to appear sophisticated leads to obvious lying. Good character beat.

jokes.text: There was an advance? - Quiet, quiet.
jokes.setup: Discussion about returning advance money
jokes.punchline: Mike revealing he didn't know there was advance money, Selina quickly shutting him up
jokes.explanation: Perfect reveal that Mike is completely out of the loop on his own ghostwriting deal. Selina's panic response is character-perfect.

jokes.text: Hanukkah. - Canaan, Rabbi. Shut up, Nikhil.
jokes.setup: Religious education question about Moses
jokes.punchline: Jonah giving completely wrong answer, then telling the correcting student to shut up
jokes.explanation: Jonah's ignorance combined with his arrogance. The wrongness of 'Hanukkah' as an answer to where Moses led people is perfectly stupid.

jokes.text: This stupid hat is too small for my head. - Yarmulke. - Fine, this stupid hat is too small for my yarmulke.
jokes.setup: Jonah complaining about yarmulke size, being corrected on terminology
jokes.punchline: Misusing the word 'yarmulke' in response
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay misuse. Jonah's complete failure to understand what he was just told is perfect character comedy.

jokes.text: Oh, good, 'cause all this learning is giving me a yarmulke ache.
jokes.setup: Jonah continuing to misuse 'yarmulke'
jokes.punchline: Using it as a substitute for 'migraine' or 'headache'
jokes.explanation: Compounds the previous joke by showing Jonah thinks 'yarmulke' means something head-related in general.

jokes.text: Oh, Jesus Christ! I'm sorry, I can say that now and still get into heaven, right? Actually, Jonah, Jews don't believe in heaven.
jokes.setup: Jonah swearing and asking if it's okay now that he's converting
jokes.punchline: Learning Jews don't believe in heaven anyway
jokes.explanation: Jonah's complete ignorance about the religion he's converting to. The irony is well-structured.

jokes.text: Shut the fuck up, Nikhil! Wait, Shawnee, how am I gonna see my dog again? Is there, like, a Jew place for dogs?
jokes.setup: Learning Jews don't believe in heaven
jokes.punchline: Immediate concern about dog afterlife and asking about 'Jew place for dogs'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah priorities - more concerned about his dog than his own soul. 'Jew place for dogs' is wonderfully stupid phrasing.

jokes.text: We're fucked. I don't even remember any of that.
jokes.setup: Looking at presidential diary entries to write book
jokes.punchline: Admitting she doesn't remember doing her job
jokes.explanation: Selina's casual admission that her presidency was so meaningless even she doesn't remember it.

jokes.text: Some things are so dull, you have to block them out. No kidding. I mean, I'm sure that I played with Catherine when she was a child... but for the life of me, I can't remember one second.
jokes.setup: Talking about blocking out dull presidential duties
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting she blocked out her own daughter's childhood
jokes.explanation: Devastatingly dark character moment. The casual equation of presidential duties with motherhood is brilliantly horrible.

jokes.text: So, is it a boy, is it a girl? - Oh, right. It's a baby. - No.
jokes.setup: Asking about baby's gender
jokes.punchline: Catherine giving the most basic non-answer possible
jokes.explanation: Catherine's complete failure to understand the question. The deadpan 'No' from Selina sells the absurdity.

jokes.text: This is a human being you're making, not some Brooklyn-based, artisanal chocolate bar.
jokes.setup: Catherine being coy about baby's gender
jokes.punchline: Comparing the baby to hipster chocolate packaging
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specificity. The Brooklyn artisanal chocolate bar perfectly captures the pretentious mystique around gender reveals.

jokes.text: Okay, is that how we're talking now, Marjorie, like some sort of bi-curious Porky Pig?
jokes.setup: Marjorie using inclusive pronouns (she/he, her/his)
jokes.punchline: Comparing it to Porky Pig's speech pattern
jokes.explanation: Absolutely brilliant comparison. The specificity of 'bi-curious Porky Pig' is comedy gold - unexpected, visual, and perfectly captures the speech pattern.

jokes.text: I had an aunt who transitioned twice. She was trapped inside a man, and then that man was trapped inside of another woman. - Oh, like a turducken.
jokes.setup: Marjorie explaining her aunt's double transition
jokes.punchline: Selina comparing it to turducken (chicken inside duck inside turkey)
jokes.explanation: Inspired comparison that makes perfect structural sense while being completely inappropriate. Selina's food-focused brain strikes again.

jokes.text: Leslie or Dana if it's a boy and... And for a girl we like Linus. - Seriously? - That feels like child abuse.
jokes.setup: Listing gender-neutral baby names
jokes.punchline: Naming a girl 'Linus', Selina calling it child abuse
jokes.explanation: The complete reversal (feminine names for boys, masculine name for girls) with Selina's harsh but accurate assessment.

jokes.text: It'll be my first human birth, so.
jokes.setup: Richard attending doula class
jokes.punchline: Specifying it's his first 'human' birth
jokes.explanation: Perfect Richard - the implication that he's attended non-human births is wonderfully strange and character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Yeah, but, I mean, does anybody really give a shit about two average-looking lesbians?
jokes.setup: Discussing Catherine's relationship for the book
jokes.punchline: Dismissing her daughter's relationship as unmarketable
jokes.explanation: Brutally honest assessment that shows Selina's complete lack of maternal feeling. The casual cruelty is very her.

jokes.text: Kent?! My snatch isn't a data port!
jokes.setup: Mike suggesting she slept with Kent
jokes.punchline: Rejecting the idea with tech metaphor
jokes.explanation: Brilliant tech metaphor that perfectly captures both Kent's robotic nature and Selina's crude directness. Highly quotable.

jokes.text: You... you said it was at the Congressional Ball. - Oh, did I say that? - I think so.
jokes.setup: Selina giving conflicting locations for her encounter
jokes.punchline: Her casual confusion about her own story
jokes.explanation: Selina's inability to keep her story straight, caught in real time. Good character moment showing her casual relationship with truth.

jokes.text: God, how can anybody jerk off to her with him sitting right there? - I'm sure you'll find a way, Congressman.
jokes.setup: Jonah commenting crudely about TV hosts
jokes.punchline: Aide's deadpan response about Jonah's determination
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response to Jonah's grossness. The aide's confidence in Jonah's perverted ingenuity is a great character beat.

jokes.text: Because we worked for the president and didn't cost her the presidency. And because we don't look like Herman Munster's brother who liked to molest that pudgy werewolf kid.
jokes.setup: Why Dan got invited and Jonah didn't
jokes.punchline: Increasingly elaborate monster metaphor for Jonah
jokes.explanation: Elaborately specific insult that escalates beautifully. The Munster family deep cut with the molesting detail is perfectly horrible.

jokes.text: Charlie, also played by Fred Gwynne. Season one, episode nine.
jokes.setup: Dan's Herman Munster reference
jokes.punchline: Kent providing the obscure TV trivia follow-up
jokes.explanation: Perfect Kent - cannot let a factual reference go uncorrected. The specificity makes it funnier.

jokes.text: No, I'm bringing my jai alai instructor. - Female. - Ish.
jokes.setup: Kent bringing a date to the unveiling
jokes.punchline: Uncertain about instructor's gender
jokes.explanation: Kent's matter-of-fact uncertainty about basic biology. The 'ish' is perfectly deadpan and strange.

jokes.text: Maybe I'm gonna go hire some women, and I'm gonna make them date me.
jokes.setup: Jonah being rejected for plus-ones
jokes.punchline: His solution involves hiring prostitutes but calling it dating
jokes.explanation: Jonah's complete misunderstanding of how dating works. The delusion that hiring women equals dating is pathetically funny.

jokes.text: And I'm Dan Egan, bringing you your morning every morning of every day for many mornings to come.
jokes.setup: Dan trying a new sign-off
jokes.punchline: Overwrought, repetitive phrasing that sounds unnatural
jokes.explanation: Dan's overconfident delivery of terrible copy. The repetition of 'morning' makes it sound ridiculous.

jokes.text: You sound like a Scientologist.
jokes.setup: Dan's weird sign-off
jokes.punchline: Specific comparison to Scientologist speech patterns
jokes.explanation: Accurate and specific comparison. The Scientologist reference perfectly captures the creepy, over-positive tone.

jokes.text: Jesus, we were not fucking! - Okay, going down on each other, whatever. I don't wanna know!
jokes.setup: Producer assuming Dan and Jane had sexual chemistry
jokes.punchline: Producer's gross misinterpretation and TMI response
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of awkwardness. The producer's casual crudeness and immediate backpedal works well.

jokes.text: But I... I'm actually fucking her. - Dan! - Aren't you engaged to a Yankee?
jokes.setup: Dan revealing his affair with Brie
jokes.punchline: Producer's casual reference to Dan's fiancée
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Dan's moral bankruptcy. The Yankee reference is a nice specific detail.

jokes.text: Maybe you two should fuck differently, then. I don't know. That's where I draw the line, man. I don't take notes on how I fuck.
jokes.setup: Producer suggesting they change their sexual chemistry
jokes.punchline: Dan's principled stand about not taking performance notes
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dan - he'll cheat on his fiancée but won't compromise his sexual technique for television. The priorities are hilariously wrong.

jokes.text: So, it's sci-fi?
jokes.setup: Tom's book title 'Investing with a Conscience'
jokes.punchline: Selina treating conscience as fictional concept
jokes.explanation: Brilliant one-liner that reveals Selina's worldview perfectly. The idea that conscience is so foreign it must be science fiction is devastatingly funny.

jokes.text: And in the end, I just turned out to be another blue bottle fly feeding on a heaping mouthful of Seabiscuit's finest.
jokes.setup: Tom giving an elaborate apology
jokes.punchline: Comparing himself to a fly eating horse manure
jokes.explanation: Overly elaborate metaphor that's both pretentious and self-deprecating. The Seabiscuit reference adds unnecessary detail that makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Oh, my God, why did I have to fuck the last remaining gentleman in Washington? - God! - Who said that? Was that Dolley Madison? - It was James Madison.
jokes.setup: Selina lamenting Tom's decency
jokes.punchline: Mike's historical correction about Madison
jokes.explanation: Good character beat showing Mike's random historical knowledge and Selina's casual crudeness. The Madison correction is perfectly pedantic.

jokes.text: Everyone was unless you're a mole person who was cast out of his underground society for keistering sewer rats. - I wasn't invited. - I know.
jokes.setup: Describing who got invited to the unveiling
jokes.punchline: Elaborate insult followed by deadpan confirmation Jonah wasn't invited
jokes.explanation: Incredibly specific and elaborate insult with perfect deadpan follow-up. The 'keistering sewer rats' detail is wonderfully gross.

jokes.text: You are addicted to spending like the proverbial drunken sailor.
jokes.setup: Political debate about spending
jokes.punchline: Using cliché but acknowledging it's a cliché
jokes.explanation: Weak political cliché that's only mildly funny because the character acknowledges it's a cliché. Below average craft.

jokes.text: Well, it's your casa white-o, as you would say.
jokes.setup: President saying it's Meyer's fiesta
jokes.punchline: Jonah's racist fake Spanish response
jokes.explanation: Jonah's casual racism and stupidity. 'Casa white-o' is perfectly wrong and offensive in character.

jokes.text: You haven't heard the last of Jonah Ryan.
jokes.setup: Jonah being dismissed from the meeting
jokes.punchline: Empty threat delivered with false confidence
jokes.explanation: Classic ineffectual villain exit line. Jonah's delusion of importance is character-appropriate but not highly original.

jokes.text: Cutting aid to Israel! - Whoa, no! We're not gonna do that. Are you crazy? - Cutting aid to veterans. - Great! - They volunteered, right?
jokes.setup: Brainstorming ways to get attention
jokes.punchline: Escalating from controversial to actively evil policy suggestions
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing the group's moral bankruptcy. The 'they volunteered' logic is perfectly twisted.

jokes.text: Okay, so I have got a White House book that is hotter than Nancy Reagan's guide to cocksucking.
jokes.setup: Selina pitching her revised book
jokes.punchline: Crude comparison involving former First Lady
jokes.explanation: Perfectly inappropriate Selina comparison. The specificity of Nancy Reagan makes it both shocking and funny.

jokes.text: She's a year older than me. - Okay, that is a fucking lie!
jokes.setup: Tom calling Selina a mentor
jokes.punchline: Selina's outrage at age implication
jokes.explanation: Classic Selina vanity moment. Her priorities (age over everything else) are perfectly in character.

jokes.text: This is like Black History Month. It never fucking ends!
jokes.setup: Realizing Tom will be at the unveiling
jokes.punchline: Comparing her problems to Black History Month
jokes.explanation: Inappropriate but character-appropriate comparison. Selina's casual insensitivity to racial issues is very her.

jokes.text: Do you want me to 69 him? - Oh, 86.
jokes.setup: Gary offering to deal with Tom
jokes.punchline: Sexual number mix-up with restaurant term
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary malapropism. The mix-up between sexual position and restaurant term is wonderfully innocent/dirty.

jokes.text: If Jackie Kennedy can go through the day with her husband's philandering brains all over her dress and her head held high, looking stunning, even though they made her ride around in a convertible like an animal, I might add, you can go to the White House tomorrow
jokes.setup: Gary giving Selina a pep talk using Jackie Kennedy
jokes.punchline: Completely inappropriate description of JFK assassination
jokes.explanation: Gary's completely tone-deaf historical comparison. 'Philandering brains' and 'like an animal' are hilariously inappropriate for such a tragic moment.

jokes.text: even though they made her ride around in a convertible like an animal, I might add
jokes.setup: Gary's inspiring Jackie Kennedy comparison
jokes.punchline: Bizarre detail about convertible being animal-like treatment
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary moment - well-meaning but with weird specific details. Good character integration.

jokes.text: where he couldn't even unzip the back of your dress. - Wait a minute, what? How do you know that? - It was in Mike's notes.
jokes.setup: Gary knowing intimate details about Selina's encounter
jokes.punchline: Revealing Mike documented everything including Tom's sexual incompetence
jokes.explanation: Perfect reveal of Mike's obsessive note-taking extending to sexual details. Gary's casual knowledge is appropriately disturbing.

jokes.text: And if one person mentions Tom James to me, I'm gonna go piss in that punch bowl.
jokes.setup: Selina agreeing to attend the unveiling
jokes.punchline: Threatening to urinate in refreshments
jokes.explanation: Classic Selina threat - crude, specific, and completely inappropriate for a White House event.

jokes.text: Well, well, well, sorry, boys, no votes today on legalizing tiny Fleshlights for micro wangs. Except for you, Gellardi. I'd wager it takes a whole roll of duct tape to strap down your hog of a cock.
jokes.setup: Jonah interrupting congressional vote
jokes.punchline: Elaborate sexual insults about colleagues
jokes.explanation: Jonah's inappropriate sexual commentary in Congress. The contrast (insulting then complimenting) works but it's crudely written.

jokes.text: Like my labradoodle, Teddy, when I cover his cock in honey and give him a sticky licky. - No, bad!
jokes.setup: Will making analogy about financial system going belly-up
jokes.punchline: Disturbing sexual detail about his dog
jokes.explanation: Deliberately shocking and inappropriate analogy. The Speaker's 'No, bad!' response like training a dog adds to the absurdity.

jokes.text: And when something's broken, you shut it down, and then you turn it back on again like with a router.
jokes.setup: Jonah explaining his government shutdown philosophy
jokes.punchline: Comparing federal government to internet router
jokes.explanation: Perfect Jonah logic - applying tech support solutions to constitutional democracy. The simplistic analogy is brilliantly stupid.

jokes.text: I know one thing... she's the one who put my sweater in the dryer. - Yes!
jokes.setup: Selina being greeted by White House staff
jokes.punchline: Her only memory of this person is a laundry mishap
jokes.explanation: Selina reducing a person's entire identity to one domestic mistake. Gary's enthusiastic agreement makes it funnier.

jokes.text: What is this Dixie cup shit show? It's like we teleported to post-Katrina Mississippi.
jokes.setup: Selina seeing the cheap refreshment setup
jokes.punchline: Comparing White House party to disaster zone
jokes.explanation: Perfectly inappropriate comparison that shows Selina's privileged perspective. The specificity of post-Katrina Mississippi is hilariously wrong.

jokes.text: Congressman Furlong. - Brookheimer! Got your dong of a résumé.
jokes.setup: Amy meeting with potential employer
jokes.punchline: Crude assessment of her résumé
jokes.explanation: Crude but effective put-down. 'Dong of a résumé' is memorably insulting if not particularly sophisticated.

jokes.text: You're stronger than me, and you have a higher reserve of dignity and self-worth. And I'm trapped in a cycle of abuse.
jokes.setup: Explaining why Amy didn't get hired
jokes.punchline: Admitting he's psychologically trapped by his boss
jokes.explanation: Dark but honest self-assessment. The casual admission of abuse cycles in political employment is grimly funny.

jokes.text: Of course I was, but I had the courtesy to seem like I might not!
jokes.setup: Tom saying she would have written about their affair anyway
jokes.punchline: Admitting he's right while claiming moral high ground for pretending otherwise
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina logic - admitting bad faith while claiming superiority for better presentation. The twisted morality is expertly crafted.

jokes.text: You've got more issues than 'National Geographic.'
jokes.setup: Tom telling Selina she needs help
jokes.punchline: Magazine-based insult about her problems
jokes.explanation: Standard 'more issues than...' joke format with magazine twist. Functional but not particularly original.

jokes.text: Oh, ho, ho! So, you did want me to call. Oh, oh, poor Tommy. He wanted me to call him afterwards.
jokes.setup: Tom revealing he's hurt she didn't call
jokes.punchline: Selina's mocking discovery of his vulnerability
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Selina finding Tom's weakness and immediately exploiting it. The mocking tone is character-perfect.

jokes.text: Yeah, well, you know what, you can have your trophy wife and your trophy office and your trophy baby, who you won't even live to see go to college, but I still got to be president, and, tsk, you never will.
jokes.setup: Selina lashing out at Tom
jokes.punchline: Wishing death on him while asserting presidential superiority
jokes.explanation: Devastatingly cruel escalation. The age-based death wish combined with presidential bragging is perfectly vicious Selina.

jokes.text: I swear to God, Tom James just wanted to fuck me right now. - What?! - Yeah, we had a real moment. But he's too much of a pussy to go for my pussy.
jokes.setup: Selina describing her encounter with Tom
jokes.punchline: Double pussy pun (coward/genitals)
jokes.explanation: Clean double entendre that's exactly Selina's style. The wordplay works well with her crude directness.

jokes.text: No, no, no, I'm not talking about the Green Room. That was just a good, old-fashioned hate fuck.
jokes.setup: Tom getting déjà vu about their interaction
jokes.punchline: Selina casually categorizing their previous sexual encounter
jokes.explanation: Selina's clinical assessment of their sexual encounter. The casual use of 'hate fuck' is perfectly inappropriate.

jokes.text: That when we were in Los Angeles and we walked out onto the stage to accept the nomination, and you grabbed my hand and you held it up high, and I was like, 'Oh, my God, he's holding my hand.'
jokes.setup: Selina confessing her feelings
jokes.punchline: Reducing major political moment to teenage-level crush
jokes.explanation: Selina revealing her presidential campaign was motivated by a schoolgirl crush. The contrast between political importance and personal triviality works well.

jokes.text: Is that her name or is that the pill you take to fuck her?
jokes.setup: Meeting Tom's wife Alethia
jokes.punchline: Comparing name to medication
jokes.explanation: Brilliant cruel improvisation. The pharmaceutical name comparison is both creative and devastating.

jokes.text: But unfortunately, I have to return to the Oval Office to deal with the shutdown.
jokes.setup: Building up to praise Selina
jokes.punchline: Immediately leaving due to Jonah's shutdown
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic payoff - Jonah's incompetence directly ruins Selina's moment. Good structural comedy.

jokes.text: You know, because maybe in a hundred years, all that anyone will remember is that you have your portrait hanging on a wall in the White House.
jokes.setup: Selina giving philosophical speech about presidential legacy
jokes.punchline: Reducing all achievement to mere wall decoration
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly nihilistic observation that captures the show's cynical worldview. The idea that achievement is just wall space is perfectly dark.

jokes.text: Yeah, that cow put the chair in the painting. - What chair? - The fucking chair in that painting is the chair that she made love to Andrew on.
jokes.setup: Looking at her official portrait
jokes.punchline: Discovering her ex-husband's affair location immortalized in art
jokes.explanation: Perfect final insult - her official portrait includes the furniture of her humiliation. The artist's petty revenge is brilliantly cruel.

jokes.text: Look at my neck. I have pardoned turkeys with fewer waddles.
jokes.setup: Critiquing her portrait
jokes.punchline: Comparing her neck to presidential turkey pardoning
jokes.explanation: Brilliant self-deprecating comparison using presidential tradition. The specificity of turkey pardoning makes it uniquely political and funny.

jokes.text: Jesus Christ, underage Muslim brides are less traumatized at their unveiling.
jokes.setup: Selina complaining about her experience
jokes.punchline: Comparing her inconvenience to child marriage
jokes.explanation: Wildly inappropriate comparison that shows Selina's complete lack of perspective. Offensive but character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Remember, every day starts with morning.
jokes.setup: Brie attempting a meaningful sign-off
jokes.punchline: Stating obvious fact as if profound
jokes.explanation: Perfect morning show vapidity. The attempt at wisdom while saying nothing meaningful captures TV banality perfectly.

tmdb_overview: Selina tries to finish her book. Selina and her staff prepare for her portrait-unveiling ceremony, while Jonah tries to weasel himself onto the guest list. Dan works on his onscreen-chemistry problem.
insight: Sustained character chaos yields 69 jokes in 39 minutes—Veep's density sweet spot.
